name: Circular Tour to the Laguna de Torrevieja via the Vía Verde
name_original: Tour circular a la Laguna de Torrevieja por la Vía Verde
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Torrevieja traverse diverse landscapes, including coastal paths, salt lagoons, and natural park wetlands. The region is characterized by its flat coastal areas and gently rolling countryside, offering varied terrain for cyclists. Notable features include the pink and blue-green salt lakes and the Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja. Greenways, often following old railway lines, provide traffic-free cycling environments. Best touring cycling routes around Torrevieja The most popular touring cycling route is Circular Tour to the Laguna de Torrevieja via the Vía Verde, a 14.9 miles (24.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique salt lagoons and follows a dedicated greenway. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop, a moderate 23.1 miles (37.2 km) path. This route navigates through the Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja, featuring wetlands and river scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Laguna Salada de Torrevieja loop from Hospital San Jaime, a 24.8 miles (39.9 km) trail leading through the pink salt lake area, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Touring cycling around Torrevieja is defined by salt lagoons, coastal promenades, and natural park wetlands. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy flat paths to routes with moderate elevation changes. The routes in Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 1000 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja and its surroundings offer a wide array of touring cycling routes, with over 115 mapped routes available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy, paved paths to more challenging options.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in Torrevieja?
A: The terrain around Torrevieja is quite diverse. You'll find flat coastal paths and promenades, gently rolling countryside, and dedicated greenways, often following old railway lines. The region is particularly known for its unique salt lagoons and natural park wetlands.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Torrevieja has many easy routes perfect for beginners and families. Approximately 38 routes are classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952443378/circular-tour-to-the-laguna-de-torrevieja-via-the-via-verde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular Tour to the Laguna de Torrevieja via the Vía Verde&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the salt lagoons on a dedicated greenway.

Q: Are there more challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists?
A: While many routes are moderate or easy, there are 23 routes classified as difficult for those seeking more of a challenge. These routes may feature more significant elevation changes or cover longer distances, extending further inland from the coast.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while cycling around Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle around the stunning pink and blue-green salt lagoons, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/771712" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt;. These areas offer unique landscapes and opportunities for birdwatching.

Q: Can I spot wildlife while cycling in Torrevieja?
A: Absolutely. The Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja is a prime spot for wildlife observation, especially birdwatching. Cyclists often see pink flamingos, gulls, herons, and grebes around the salt lagoons. There are even dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Huts&lt;/a&gt; within the park.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, many routes in Torrevieja are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952445615/laguna-de-la-mata-and-rio-segura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Mata and Río Segura Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the natural park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31417991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Salada de Torrevieja loop from Hospital San Jaime&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja benefits from favorable weather conditions year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even winter cycling is popular due to the mild climate, while summer can be enjoyed, especially during cooler morning or late afternoon hours.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Torrevieja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 1000 reviews. Reviewers often praise the unique landscapes of the salt lagoons, the well-maintained greenways, and the variety of routes suitable for all abilities.

Q: Are there options for bike rental in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, equipment rental services are readily available in Torrevieja, making it easy for visitors to explore the area on two wheels without bringing their own bikes. You can find various types of bicycles to suit your touring needs.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: While the region is known for its natural beauty, you can also encounter historical elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4101670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeo Caves&lt;/a&gt; are a notable attraction, and some routes, like the Ruta de las Eras de la Sal, take you through rural areas where you can observe historic salt pits and windmills.

Q: Can I cycle along the coast in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, Torrevieja offers several coastal cycling options. The Ronda Litoral is a popular route along the Mediterranean coast, and the Paseo Marítimo Juan Aparicio provides another ideal promenade for a coastal ride, offering views of beaches and the waterfront.
tips: "Vias Verdes de Torrevieja" - fun to drive this way. Pot level and pleasantly wide.
Worthwhile detour from the coast inland. Among locals and few tourists, you can comfortably enjoy your espresso in the old town.

waypoints.name: Vía Verde de Torrevieja – La Mata and Torrevieja Lagoons Nature Park
